title: rubus arduennensis var. angustatus
intro: Rubus arduennensis var. angustatus, also known as the narrow-leaved bramble, is a variety of the Rubus arduennensis plant, which is a type of thorny shrub belonging to the rose family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus arduennensis var. angustatus include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using the berries to create a jam or syrup, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
